name: Canada Day
description: July 1, the maple leaf, Confederation, and the holiday formerly known as Dominion Day.

questions.q: On what date is Canada Day celebrated each year?
What was Canada Day originally called from 1867 to 1982?
In what year was Dominion Day officially renamed to Canada Day?

source.notes: Same year the Constitution was patriated.

questions.q: What 1867 act united the British colonies into the Dominion of Canada?
Which were the original four provinces of Canada in 1867?
choices: Ontario, Quebec, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick
Nova Scotia, PEI, Newfoundland, New Brunswick

source.original_a: United Canadas, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick
source.notes: The 'Province of Canada' was split into Ontario and Quebec, joining Nova Scotia and New Brunswick.

questions.q: Which leaf is featured on the Canadian flag?

source.notes: An 11-pointed stylized red sugar maple leaf — adopted in 1965.

questions.q: What is the title of Canada's national anthem?

source.notes: Composed in 1880; officially adopted as the national anthem in 1980.

questions.q: What year did Canada celebrate its centennial (100th) anniversary of Confederation?

source.notes: The Order of Canada was also created on July 1, 1967.

questions.q: What anniversary did Canada celebrate in 2017?
What is the name of Canada's highest civilian honour, founded on July 1, 1967?

source.notes: Recognizes outstanding achievement and service to the country.

questions.q: Where are the main national Canada Day celebrations held each year?

source.notes: Moved to LeBreton Flats in 2022 during Parliament restoration.

questions.q: Which Canadian Forces aerobatic team frequently performs on Canada Day?
What is Canada's official summer national sport?

source.notes: Both are official: Lacrosse (summer) and Hockey (winter), per the National Sports of Canada Act, 1994.

questions.q: What is Canada's official winter national sport?
Which act passed in 1982 patriated the Canadian Constitution from the UK Parliament?

source.notes: Brought the Constitution under fully Canadian control and added the Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

questions.q: Which Canadian province observes a moving / lease-changing day on July 1, often coinciding with Canada Day?

source.notes: Most residential leases in Quebec traditionally run July 1 to June 30.

questions.q: On July 1, 1916, the Royal Newfoundland Regiment suffered devastating losses at which WWI battle?

source.notes: Newfoundland (then a separate Dominion) observes July 1 as Memorial Day for that reason.

questions.q: Which classical Canadian Day broadcast began in 1927 and was hosted by the Canadian National Railway?
Which year did Canada Day celebrations go fully virtual due to the COVID-19 pandemic?
Where do Canadian expatriates in London (UK) traditionally gather for Canada Day celebrations?
